task_completion.description: The agent opens the 'New Network Link' dialog box.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pop-up window titled 'New Network Link' or 'Google Earth - New Network Link' is visible.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the correct name for the Network Link.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Name' field in the dialog contains the text 'USGS Earthquakes M2.5+ Past Day'.

task_completion.description: The agent enters the correct KML URL.
The new Network Link appears in the Places panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'USGS Earthquakes M2.5+ Past Day' is visible in the sidebar under the 'Places' or 'My Places' section.
The checkbox next to 'USGS Earthquakes M2.5+ Past Day' in the Places panel is checked/ticked.

task_completion.description: Earthquake markers are rendered on the globe.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Icons, markers, or colored dots representing earthquakes are visible on the 3D map view.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the Google Earth application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Google Earth Pro interface (globe, sidebar, toolbar) rather than a web browser or code editor.

integrity.description: The agent uses the native dialog to create the link.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'New Network Link' dialog is observed, ensuring the agent didn't just edit a KML file externally.

integrity.description: The result is viewed in the application, not a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The earthquake data is displayed on the application's 3D globe, not as raw XML/KML text in a web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is Google Earth (likely Pro). The expected Network Link name is 'USGS Earthquakes M2.5+ Past Day'. The expected URL is 'https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/summary/2.5_day.kml'.
